result:
Implemented a calendar-driven journal view in the web UI.

In [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go), I added a `/journal` route, journal-specific view models, and snapshot indexing for daily notes by detecting note IDs in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. The journal page now renders a month grid, marks days with existing entries, lets you move to previous/next month, and includes a `Today` jump. Selecting a day shows that day’s note inline when it exists, or an empty state when it does not. I also added a `Journal` button to the shared sidebar toolbar so the feature is reachable from the rest of the UI.

In [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go), I updated the server tests to use the current `newServeMux(server)` API and added coverage for the journal page: one test for rendering a populated daily entry with calendar markers and month navigation, and one for the missing-entry empty state.

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` in this environment because `gofmt` and `go` are not installed on `PATH`.
